response: 193 is prime. No prime factors up to √193 ≈ 13.9 divide it.
reasoning_content: We need to check if 193 is prime. A quick sanity check: test divisibility by small primes. 193 is odd, not divisible by 2. Sum of digits 1+9+3=13, not divisible by 3. Last digit not 0 or 5, so not divisible by 5. Check 7: 7*27=189, remainder 4, so not divisible. 11: 11*17=187, remainder 6, not divisible. 13: 13*14=182, remainder 11, not divisible. 17: 17*11=187, remainder 6, not divisible. 19: 19*10=190, remainder 3, not divisible. Next prime is 23: 23*8=184, remainder 9. 29: 29*6=174, remainder 19. 31: 31*6=186, remainder 7. 37: 37*5=185, remainder 8. 41: 41*4=164, remainder 29. 43: 43*4=172, remainder 21. 47: 47*4=188, remainder 5. 53: 53*3=159, remainder 34. 59: 59*3=177, remainder 16. 61: 61*3=183, remainder 10. 67: 67*2=134, remainder 59. 71: 71*2=142, remainder 51. 73: 73*2=146, remainder 47. 79: 79*2=158, remainder 35. 83: 83*2=166, remainder 27. 89: 89*2=178, remainder 15. 97: 97*1=97, remainder 96, but 97^2=9409 > 193, so we only need to check primes up to sqrt(193) ≈ 13.89. Actually sqrt(193) is about 13.89, so we need to check primes up to 13. We already checked 2,3,5,7,11,13. None divide. So 193 is prime. So answer: yes, prime.
